title: Name the condition under which 'the moat is durable' flips
guidance: Reward a concrete, decision-relevant flip condition. Penalise vagueness.

runner_input.instruction: Add the 'this only holds if…' line to the moat argument below.
runner_input.constraints: One sentence. Names a concrete, checkable condition.

source_blocks.text: Halden's moat is institutional: 28 years of IFSM endorsement, recognition by BRCGS auditors and EHOs, and 14 QMS integrations that consume the library. An LLM can draft a plausible schedule, but it cannot manufacture that endorsement or auditor acceptance, so the moat is durable against AI.

rubric_notes: Good condition: 'this only holds if the buyer's reason to purchase is audit assurance; if it shifts to merely a high-quality schedule, pricing comes under pressure.'
Also acceptable: holds only if the QMS integration contracts prevent content reuse and IFSM exclusivity stays unilateral.
